product_overview: A small ASP.NET Core MVC application to manage Events (CRUD) backed by MySQL and EF Core, containerized with Docker for deployment to a VPS with a custom domain and SSL.
core_goals: Deliver a functioning CRUD web app for events (Create, Read, Update, Delete).
Containerize the application and provide a reproducible VPS deployment flow (Docker + domain + SSL).
Follow professional patterns for a learning project: DTO usage, async EF Core calls, and EF migrations.
Ensure the app runs locally, builds as a Docker image, and is reachable on the VPS via HTTPS.

features.name: Event Management (CRUD)
features.description: Create, read, update and delete Event records via MVC endpoints. Uses CreateEventDto and EditEventDto with ModelState validation and async EF Core operations.
user_flows: GET /Event/Index -> Receive 200 with list of events rendered in view (happy path)

GET /Event/Create -> Receive 200 with empty create form -> POST /Event/Create with body {Title: "Summer Gala", Img: "https://.../img.jpg", Description: "Annual event", Location: "Town Hall", Status: "Active"} -> Receive 302 redirect to /Event/Index -> GET /Event/Index -> Receive 200 with new event displayed (create happy path)

POST /Event/Create with body {Img: "https://.../img.jpg", Description: "Missing title", Location: "Hall", Status: "Active"} -> Receive 200 with validation errors in view (create error: ModelState invalid / missing required fields)

GET /Event/Edit/1 -> Receive 200 with edit form populated for event id=1 -> POST /Event/Edit/1 with body {Id:1, Title: "Updated", Img: "...", Description: "Updated desc", Location: "Hall", Status: "Active"} -> Receive 302 redirect to /Event/Index -> GET /Event/Index -> Receive 200 with updated event (edit happy path)
GET /Event/Edit/9999 -> Receive 404 NotFound when event id does not exist (edit error path)
POST /Event/Edit/9999 with body {Id:9999, Title:"X", Img:"...", Description:"...", Location:"...", Status:"Active"} -> Receive 404 NotFound (edit POST error when id missing)
POST /Event/Edit/1 with body missing required fields {Id:1, Img:"..."} -> Receive 200 with validation errors in view (edit validation error)
POST /Event/Delete/1 -> Receive 302 redirect to /Event/Index -> GET /Event/Index -> Receive 200 without deleted event (delete happy path - current implementation performs hard delete)
POST /Event/Delete/9999 -> Receive 404 NotFound when event id does not exist (delete error path)
GET /Event/Index -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error (DB connection failure) (list error path describing known gap: no global DB error handling)

features.name: Database & Migrations
features.description: EF Core migrations to apply the Event schema to MySQL. Connection string is assembled from environment variables (DB_SERVER, DB_NAME, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD).
user_flows: dotnet ef migrations add InitialMigration --project <project> -> Receive success with new migration files generated -> dotnet ef database update --project <project> -> Receive success applying migration and creating Events table (migrations happy path)
dotnet ef database update --connection "Server=wrong;Uid=..;Pwd=..;Database=.." -> Receive error about DB connection or authentication failure (migration error path due to invalid connection string)
Run SQL query SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Events via MySQL client -> Receive result with event rows (verification after migration)
Run SQL query SHOW TABLES when migrations not applied -> Receive empty/no Events table (verification error path)

features.name: Dockerization & VPS Deployment
features.description: Two-stage Dockerfile builds and runs the ASP.NET Core app on container port 8080. Deploy to VPS by building image on server, running container with environment variables, and configuring DNS + SSL for custom domain.
user_flows: docker build -t vps:latest . -> Receive success (image built using multi-stage Dockerfile)

docker run -e DB_SERVER=... -e DB_NAME=... -e DB_USER=... -e DB_PASSWORD=... -p 8080:8080 vps:latest -> Container starts with ASP.NET listening on 8080 -> curl http://localhost:8080/Event/Index -> Receive 200 with event list (container runtime happy path)

Push image to registry -> SSH to VPS -> docker pull registry/vps:latest -> docker run -e ... -p 8080:8080 registry/vps:latest -> Configure DNS A record for example.com to VPS IP -> curl https://example.com/Event/Index -> Receive 200 with valid TLS (deployment happy path)
docker run without setting DB_* env vars -> curl http://localhost:8080/Event/Index -> Receive 500 due to DB connection error (runtime error path: missing runtime env vars)
Configure HTTPS but forget to set up certificate -> curl https://example.com -> Receive TLS/SSL certificate error (deployment error path)

features.name: Event Management
features.description: CRUD operations for Event resources via MVC controller

response_schema.200: List of Event objects rendered in View

endpoints.description: Create a new event from form submission
Render edit form for a specific event

response_schema.200: Edit form view with event data

endpoints.description: Delete a specific event by id

known_limitations.issue: No authentication or authorization on any endpoint
known_limitations.impact: Any user can create, edit, or delete events

known_limitations.issue: Hard delete instead of soft delete using the existing Deleted status
known_limitations.impact: Data is permanently removed; Deleted status enum value is unused
All events are loaded into memory at once; could fail at scale
